title: presla arvensis
intro: Presla arvensis, also known as field balsam, is a perennial plant species native to Europe and Asia. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and sore throats, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant's leaves and flowers are used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its health benefits, which include relieving congestion, reducing fever, and soothing digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Presla arvensis are also used to treat skin conditions and wounds, thanks to its antimicrobial and antiseptic properties.
